fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: Various decorative elements soften the impact of this outfit’s simple and severe style. They include the long thick row of buttons, the long glove in soft kid leather, plus the silk organza roses knotted at wrist with a topstitched ribbon.
fields.description: Jacket in dark blue wool satin. Round neck, long slim hip-skimming style. Strong shoulders with radial arm construction and overlay slit cuff. Slanted double besom flap pockets. Thick front buttoning with mannish flair. From upper hip to bottom hem a delicate flaring with buttons & buttonholes solely ornamental. Skirt in dark blue wool satin. Pencil cut, knee length, center slit front & back. Shirt in a natural hue of white silk cady. Short sleeves, crossover front, hip-skimming length.
fields.materials: Wool satin (Sisan, Milano; art. 12605, 100% wool, col. navy blue), silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, 100% silk, col. white)

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: The traditionally simple notion of shirtwaist takes on a charmingly fresh flair thanks to the clever use of bias cuts and flat pleats to give the dress a swingy feel. Another interesting feature is the opposition between polkadot prints: irregular on a cyan & navy blue base for the crepe de chine; small (1 cm dia) and random on a navy blue base, complete with white horizontal 8 cm parallel striping, for the silk mousseline.
fields.description: Dress in crepe de chine, mannish shirtwaist style with buttoning down front and small fabric-covered buttons. Padded shoulders, shirt type cuffs. Fluid line with vertical cuts to knee, from there wedge-shaped inserts on the bias. Silk mousseline doubling with flat pleats on bottom part, at same level as exterior bias-cut inserts. Skirt in dark blue silk satin. Pencil cut, knee length.
fields.materials: Crepe de chine (Distel, Paris; art. 44/154, des. 5185, 100% silk), silk print mousseline (Stucchi, Como; art. 5126, des. 40546, 100% silk)

Dress in crepe de chine, mannish shirtwaist style with buttoning down front and small fabric-covered buttons. Padded
shoulders, shirt type cuffs. Fluid line with vertical cuts to knee, from there wedge-shaped inserts on the bias. Silk mousseline
doubling with flat pleats on bottom part, at same level as exterior bias-cut inserts.
Skirt in dark blue silk satin. Pencil cut, knee length.
The traditionally simple notion of shirtwaist takes on a charmingly fresh flair thanks to the clever use of bias cuts and flat
pleats to give the dress a swingy feel. Another interesting feature is the opposition between polkadot prints: irregular on a
cyan & navy blue base for the crepe de chine; small (1 cm dia) and random on a navy blue base, complete with white
horizontal 8 cm parallel striping, for the silk mousseline.

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: This creation highlights the designer’s unique ability to fashion clothes as if pieces of sculpture. The trick is in the apparently simple sartorial solutions – bordering on the origami technique and as such in the finest Ferré tradition.
fields.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
fields.description: Dress in dark blue wool, knee length. Two-tier construction: a slim tube style on the bottom, a sleeveless halter style on top. Adding to the beauty of the bodice part is a high collar open in front down to waist. It folds back to form a strapless bustier with three-button fastening midway. A similar folding process marks the otherwise open-back dress.
fields.materials: Wool poplin (Agnona, Biella; art. 32480/1; col. blue 02509, 100% wool)

Dress in dark blue wool, knee length. Two-tier construction: a slim tube style on the bottom, a sleeveless halter style on
top. Adding to the beauty of the bodice part is a high collar open in front down to waist. It folds back to form a strapless
bustier with three-button fastening midway. A similar folding process marks the otherwise open-back dress.
This creation highlights the designer’s unique ability to fashion clothes as if pieces of sculpture. The trick is in the apparently
simple sartorial solutions – bordering on the origami technique and as such in the finest Ferré tradition.
FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: Here the defining factor is the dynamic quality of forms, as if blown by a sudden gust of wind: the skirt hugs the body in back, mimics a ripple effect in front. The sense of opulence comes from a simple intuition: namely, doubling up the panels and playing with pleats to give volume to a flat geometric shape.
fields.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
fields.description: Jacket in dark blue wool satin. An (upper) hip-skimming funnel shape. Lavish lapel collar with single-button closure at waist, kimono style sleeve snug in the wrist. Skirt in heavy silk herringbone-weave jacquard with regular black&white striping. Slim tubular line, knee length. A balloon effect thanks to two deeply pleated and folded back panels sewn onto the skirt at waistband level. Bustier in dark blue wool satin. Vertical strapless cut, waist length.
fields.exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
fields.materials: Wool satin (Sisan, Milano; art. 12605 col. blue); b&w stripe silk jacquard (Sisan, Milano; art. 4382)

kimono style sleeve snug in the wrist.
Skirt in heavy silk herringbone-weave jacquard with regular black&white striping. Slim tubular line, knee length. A balloon
effect thanks to two deeply pleated and folded back panels sewn onto the skirt at waistband level.
Bustier in dark blue wool satin. Vertical strapless cut, waist length.
Here the defining factor is the dynamic quality of forms, as if blown by a sudden gust of wind: the skirt hugs the body in
back, mimics a ripple effect in front. The sense of opulence comes from a simple intuition: namely, doubling up the panels
and playing with pleats to give volume to a flat geometric shape.
FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: The “capote de paseo” – cape the torero uses during a bullfight – is the source of inspiration for this outfit, where telling the lead story is the contrast between the severity elegance of the dress and the slinky opulence of the cape.
fields.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
fields.description: Bustier dress faux two-piece style: strapless white cotton piqué bodice low cut in back; slim crepe de chine skirt with lilac crystal beading. Black silk 8 cm wide belt with matching vertical rectangular buckle. Cape in lilac silk gazaar. In large oval shape folded in two asymmetrically lengthwise so that the different size ruffles drape naturally.
fields.materials: Cotton piqué knit, crêpe de chine, silk gazaar (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 1657), crystal beads

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: The contrast between sweet spring bouquet and slim spiral skirt is what captures the imagination here.
fields.description: Long skirt in white silk cady. Spiral construction where wide band of doubled up fabric crisscrosses under bust, hugs hips, then creates a wrap-around effect all the way down. Top in white silk cady with adhesive element. Band type cut offset by diapason at mid front neckline. Bolero in white silk cady. Basically a shell with funnel sleeves. Thick application randomly all over of silk gazar and organza flowers in various shapes, sizes and colors. Small bias-cut ruffles plus cloth leaves & stems heighten the big bouquet sensation. Topstitched black ribbon knotted into a half bow.
fields.working_process: Hand application of silk flowers all over
fields.materials: Silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), silk organza, silk gazar

Bolero in white silk cady. Basically a shell with funnel sleeves. Thick application randomly all over of silk gazar and organza
flowers in various shapes, sizes and colors. Small bias-cut ruffles plus cloth leaves & stems heighten the big bouquet

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: An apparently simple crewneck dress finds new definition thanks to the trumpet skirt effect. It’s a master stroke. Adding to the upbeat appeal and the swingy feel are the bias cuts, the sunburst pleats, the playful prints and the small multicolor sequins.
fields.description: Dress in multicolor confetti print silk chiffon (fuchsia, red, yellow, turquoise, green, royal blue, white). Ankle-length siren style with round neck, straight padded shoulders and slim elbow sleeves. Soft chromatic degradé effect along hem. Two-part construction: an outer layer with vertical parallel cuts from shoulder to knee, from there wedge-shaped inserts on the bias; an inner layer with sunburst pleats peeking out from two slits at mid front and mid back, respectively.
fields.materials: Confetti print silk chiffon (Stucchi, Como; art. 5113, des. 40549, var. 001, 100% silk), silk chiffon in polkadot print (Sisan, Milano; art. 68 G/G, des. 1049, 100% silk), sequins

Dress in multicolor confetti print silk chiffon (fuchsia, red, yellow, turquoise, green, royal blue, white). Ankle-length siren
style with round neck, straight padded shoulders and slim elbow sleeves. Soft chromatic degradé effect along hem. Two-part
construction: an outer layer with vertical parallel cuts from shoulder to knee, from there wedge-shaped inserts on the bias;
an inner layer with sunburst pleats peeking out from two slits at mid front and mid back, respectively.
An apparently simple crewneck dress finds new definition thanks to the trumpet skirt effect. It’s a master stroke. Adding to
the upbeat appeal and the swingy feel are the bias cuts, the sunburst pleats, the playful prints and the small multicolor
sequins.

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: Here the impression is of a sinuous female body blossoming from a bustier and an elementary skirt. The corolla effect of the bodice derives from a masterly manipulation of geometric figures (cones and truncated cones). The dress also has the feel of a column with capital.
fields.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
fields.description: Dress with white silk gazaar bodice, long straight black silk cady skirt. Yellow silk ribbon belt with decorative topstitching. The bodice consists of a strapless bustier with two mostly overlapping stand-up truncated cone elements set at waist. Other circular pieces are folded over and inserted in back to create a corolla or ruffle effect
fields.exhibitions: “1951-2001. Made in Italy Memoria”, Palazzo della Triennale, Milano, 2001
fields.materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, col. white, 100% silk), silk cady (Stucchi, Como; art. 5115, 100% silk)

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Here too it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
fields.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
fields.description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with dark blue 4 cm wide stripes (20 cm spacing). In reality, forming this skirt are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in dark blue wool/silk. Slim line, knee length. Top in dark blue wool/silk with black glass tube beading. Slim fit, round neck. Totally open back with crisscrossing ribbon straps. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
fields.materials: Cotton piqué in horizontal stripe print (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1055), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. blue), silk organza, glass tube beads

cloth – one the silk jacquard, the other a plain tone silk gazar – softly gathered into six groups of pleats at the waist. Open at
mid front, all the way down to hem.
Short skirt in navy blue wool/silk. Slim line, knee length. Allover black (vertical) glass tube beading.
The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Here too it’s all
about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
28/07/2014

fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Again, it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
fields.description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with navy blue striping. Creating the voluminous effect are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in black wool/silk satin. Slim line, knee length. T-shirt in wool/silk with navy blue glass tube beading. Slim fit, ultra low-cut V neck, elbow-length sleeves. While the alignment of the tube beads is mostly vertical, along the neckline and the lower edge of sleeves it is horizontal, so forming a border. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
fields.materials: White cotton piqué with navy blue striping (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1036), silk organza, wool/silk satin (Taroni, art. 17055, col. black)

Long skirt in white cotton piqué with navy blue striping. Creating the voluminous effect are two fully overlapping pieces of
cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to
hem.
Short skirt in black wool/silk satin. Slim line, knee length.
T-shirt in wool/silk with navy blue glass tube beading. Slim fit, ultra low-cut V neck, elbow-length sleeves. While the
alignment of the tube beads is mostly vertical, along the neckline and the lower edge of sleeves it is horizontal, so forming a
border.
Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Again, it’s all
about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
